cards.back: A software testing method where the tester has no access to the internal workings of the system, but only knows the inputs and expected outputs.
A software testing method where the tester has access to the internal workings of the system, allowing for a more thorough examination of the code.
A software testing method that combines elements of both black box and white box testing, where the tester has some access to the internal workings of the system.
An iterative and incremental software development approach that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
A software testing method where individual units of code are tested in isolation to ensure they function as expected.
